metadata.name: Create and Manage File Systems on Standard Partitions
metadata.description: Create a partition using the MBR partition scheme, format it with XFS, and persistently mount it
objectives: Create a partition by using the MBR partition scheme
Format the partition with the XFS file system

hosts.name: servera
hosts.description: Target server for partition and file system management

tasks.name: Verify MBR partition table on /dev/sdb
tasks.description: The /dev/sdb disk should have an msdos (MBR) partition table
tasks.success_message: MBR partition table is configured on /dev/sdb
tasks.failure_message: MBR partition table is not configured on /dev/sdb
hints: Create MBR partition table with: sudo parted /dev/sdb mklabel msdos

tasks.name: Verify /dev/sdb1 partition exists
tasks.description: A primary partition /dev/sdb1 should exist on the disk
hints: Create partition with parted: sudo parted /dev/sdb mkpart primary xfs 2048s 1001MB

tasks.name: Verify XFS filesystem on /dev/sdb1
tasks.description: The /dev/sdb1 partition should be formatted with XFS
hints: If already formatted, you may need to use -f flag to force

tasks.name: Verify /archive directory exists
tasks.description: The /archive mount point directory should exist

tasks.name: Verify /etc/fstab entry for /archive
tasks.description: An entry for /archive mount point should exist in /etc/fstab
tasks.failure_message: fstab entry for /archive is missing or incorrect
hints: Run: sudo systemctl daemon-reload after editing fstab

tasks.name: Verify /archive is mounted
tasks.description: The /archive directory should have the filesystem mounted
hints: Or mount all fstab entries: sudo mount -a
